behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is template-lint logic itself (string/AST rule check), not module handoff. Adversarial critique rejected: do not pick Integration just because skills/files exist elsewhere. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text "static prompt-template lint check" (no real OS/network/subprocess, no model call).
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint rule with a prompt template fixture that includes a verbatim phase instruction copied from SKILL.md and assert it fails with a precise violation location/message; include a clean template control that passes.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundaries: interpolation + fixture merge + prompt assembly. Unit is too low because leakage can be introduced during handoff. Rejection gate: System/Agentic disqualified by behavior text (assembled prompt content check only; no required real subprocess/network or model judgment).
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly path with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, then assert the final prompt text has no forbidden phase-instruction fragments from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This is wiring/state-origin correctness across loader + harness + validator; mocking only one function can miss hard-coded fallback paths. Integration brake checks: no required real network/subprocess; no required model-output quality assertion in this behavior text. Rejection gate: Agentic disqualified because behavior targets source-of-truth loading, not LLM decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the harness with real plugin/skill file inputs containing a unique sentinel instruction, keep prompt rules generic, and assert validation traces behavior to loaded skill context (and fails when sentinel is absent).

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Needed failure boundary is harness configuration + file variant selection + prompt invariance across runs. Bogus shortcut rejected: not Agentic by default just because this feature area involves LLMs. Rejection gate: higher levels disqualified because text requires run-capability and unchanged prompt text, not model-quality outcomes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two harness runs with identical user-style prompt text: one with baseline SKILL.md and one with degraded/missing variant. Assert run setup succeeds in both and exact prompt text is unchanged while skill source differs.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior changing when skill guidance regresses; deterministic stubs can always pass and hide the failure (deterministic-assertion trap). Rejection gate: Workflow disqualified by behavior text describing a single behavioral test outcome, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model test twice with identical user-style prompt: baseline skill vs degraded/removed relevant section. Assert baseline passes and degraded variant fails on the same behavioral criterion.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering/hand-off across response capture, artifact writer, and parser modules. Unit may miss pipeline order bugs. Rejection gate: Agentic disqualified because assertion is about capture order, not output quality; System disqualified because no special OS/network behavior is required to verify ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real harness pipeline with a deterministic model response fixture, then assert artifact contains unmodified raw output and timestamp/order evidence shows capture occurred before parser/extractor invocation.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is assertion logic semantics. A single malformed output can prove keyword checks pass while structural checks fail. Adversarial critique rejected: do not escalate to Integration by default for multi-module features when behavior is assertion algorithm correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test assertion helpers with fixtures: (a) keyword-present but wrong schema/role placement must fail, (b) correctly structured payload must pass, (c) missing required path must fail with targeted error.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution and depends on what the model actually returns; mocks hide this regression class. Integration brake: LLM-output dependence is yes, so Integration is insufficient. Rejection gate: Workflow disqualified because this describes one live response check, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke tests with structural assertions enabled and keyword-only comparator in diagnostics; demonstrate a real regression sample where keyword check would pass but structural assertion fails.
